# CWE-1204 positive: static AES-CBC initialization vector (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-1204-vulnerable.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

// CWE-1204: Generation of Weak Initialization Vector (IV).
// Vulnerable: cipher.NewCBCEncrypter reuses fixed iv bytes for every request.

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"encoding/base64"
	"net/http"
)

var weakIVPure = []byte("1234567890123456")

func EncryptFieldPure(key []byte) http.HandlerFunc {
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		if err := r.ParseForm(); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		plain := r.FormValue("value")
		block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
		if err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
			return
		}
		mode := cipher.NewCBCEncrypter(block, weakIVPure)
		padded := pkcs7PadPure([]byte(plain), block.BlockSize())
		out := make([]byte, len(padded))
		mode.CryptBlocks(out, padded)
		encoded := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(out)
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		w.Write([]byte(`{"ciphertext":"` + encoded + `"}`))
	}
}

func pkcs7PadPure(data []byte, blockSize int) []byte {
	pad := blockSize - len(data)%blockSize
	padding := make([]byte, pad)
	for i := range padding {
		padding[i] = byte(pad)
	}
	return append(data, padding...)
}
